 SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition:Use Configuration activity ID 107322 (Activate Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions) in your configuration environment instead of the transaction and SM30 view mentioned in the steps below. All other steps remain the same.

Prerequisites

  • You have access to the SAP system with authorization to maintain customizing views (or the configuration environment for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition).
  • You have downloaded the latest version of the XP Z12-012 standard from the tax authority portal (see Section 8 for instructions).

Steps 

Step 1: Access the configuration view

  • SAP ERP / SAP S/4HANA / SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Run transaction EDOC_FR_SI_REASONCOD, or run SM30 and enter view name EDOFRSICODMOTIFV
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: In your configuration environment, search for Configuration activity 107322 (Activate Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions)

Step 2: Create new entries

  • Click "New Entries"
  • Enter the Reason Code, use the official code identifier from the XP Z12-012 Excel file (e.g., CMD_ERR, REF_ERR). The complete list with all valid codes is provided in Section 5 of this blog.
  • Enter the Reason Code Description; a meaningful label in your preferred language
  • Check the action checkboxes (Accept, Reject, PartAccept, Suspend, Dispute) based on the applicability matrix in Section 5. The ✓ marks in that table indicate which actions each reason code is officially valid for, as defined by the French tax authority standard.

Step 3: Save your entries

  • Click Save
  • The entries are stored in Activate Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions (EDOFRSICODMOTIF)

7. Entering Reason Codes and Action Codes in your system

Now that you have configured the reason codes and, since the action codes are pre-delivered by SAP, the system is ready to capture this information from the user at the right moment.

7.1  Which Actions Are Available Depends on the Document Status

The actions available to you in the eDocument Cockpit (Accept, Reject, Dispute, Suspend and Partially Accept) depend on the current status of the electronic document. For example, a document in Suspended status allows you to trigger Accept, Reject, Dispute or Partially Accept. A document in Rejected status allows you to trigger Accept, Dispute, Suspend or Partially Accept. A document in Accepted status has no further actions available, as it is a final status.

To see the complete list of statuses and which actions are possible for each, you can check directly in the eDocument Cockpit:

  1. Select Domestic Supplier Invoice process
  2. Click Help
  3. Click the link Process Statuses for Domestic Supplier Invoice

This opens the built-in system documentation with full status and action matrix for the Domestic Supplier Invoice process.

7.2  Pop-up Behaviour When an Action Is Triggered

When an action is triggered for an electronic document in the eDocument Cockpit, such as Accept, Reject, Dispute, Suspend, or Partially Accept, the system displays a pop-up asking for relevant data (Reason Code, Action Code, or both) based on your configuration.

If you have configured reason codes for that action, the pop-up will ask for both reason code and an action code. If no reason codes are configured for that action, the pop-up will ask only for an action code.

For example: if you click Dispute but have not configured any reason codes for the Dispute action, the pop-up will show only the Action Code field. It will not ask for a Reason Code. Once you configure reason codes for Dispute (as described in Section 3), the pop-up will show both fields.

8. Where to Find the Official Code Lists (XP Z12-012)

The official reason codes and action codes are published by the French tax authority as part of the XP Z12-012 standard. Follow these steps to download the official Excel file:

  1. Go to: https://www.impots.gouv.fr/specifications-externes-b2b
  2. Navigate to the AFNOR standard page for XP Z12-012

     

     3. Click "Consultation gratuite" (Free Consultation)

     4.Select "Consulter en Anglais" (or French)

     5. Click "Téléchargement des annexes" (Download Annexes)

     6. Extract the ZIP file. It contains the Excel file XP_Z12-012_Annexe_A_2026_V1.3.xlsx

     7. Open the Excel file and navigate to:

  • "Codes Action" tab → Action codes (already pre-delivered by SAP; for reference only)
  • "Tableau des motifs de STATUTS" tab → Reason codes matrix with applicable statuses (use this to configure Section 3)

9. Key Takeaways

Key Takeaways Table

Topic

Summary

Reason Codes

Must be configured by the customer using 'Activate Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions (transaction EDOC_FR_SI_REASONCOD)'. Use the XP Z12-012 standard ( Section 8 ) as your reference.

Action Codes

Pre-delivered by SAP. No configuration needed. Aligned with the official XP Z12-012 standard.

Pop-up behavior

Shows Reason Code + Action Code fields if reason codes are configured for the action; shows only Action Code otherwise.

Reject & Tax Authority

Only the Reject action status is transmitted to the French tax authority (DGFiP), this is a legal mandate. All other actions are communicated only to the sender access point.

Maintenance

The tax authority may update the reason code list. Monitor the XP Z12-012 standard and update your SAP configuration accordingly.

 

10. Related SAP Help Portal Documentation

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Processing Electronic Domestic Supplier Invoices
  • Activating Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Processing Electronic Domestic Supplier Invoices
  • Activating Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions

SAP ERP

  • Processing Electronic Domestic Supplier Invoices
  • Activating Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions

I hope you find this information useful. You can leave a comment on this blog or follow us for more information about SAP Document and Reporting Compliance here in SAP Community.

Author: Mahesh Varma Kalidindi | SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

entry.title: E-Invoices are Changing Everything in Tax Compliance - Here's How to Stay Ahead
entry.content:
Tax authorities around the world are moving fast. E-invoicing mandates are expanding, reporting requirements are tightening, and the bar for real-time compliance is rising. For finance and IT teams, the pressure is mounting - and the old ways of managing invoices and tax compliance simply aren't keeping up.                                                                                                                                         

Here's what's changing, why it's getting harder, and how organizations can turn compliance into a competitive advantage.                 

 

What Is Actually Changing                                                                                                                           

An e-invoice is no longer just a digital version of a paper invoice. It has become a government-enforced exchange and processing framework, with country-specific formats, standards, and submission workflows that must be followed precisely.  Regulatory models can vary significantly by country:                                                                                    

  • Interoperability models (e.g., Belgium) allow exchange for example through Peppol network
  • E-reporting models (e.g., Hungary) require registration of invoice data with authorities after sending invoices to buyers
  • Clearance models (e.g., Mexico) route invoices through tax authorities in real time before they can be considered valid
  • Centralized exchange models (e.g., Italy, Poland) use government-managed platforms to exchange invoices between supplier and buyers
  • 5-corner models (e.g., France, UAE, Oman) introduce additional distribution layers via regulated networks like Peppol network and submission of tax data to authorities within the same flow.   

On top of this, tax authorities are getting smarter with the data they collect. Using, for example, electronic invoices, online cash registers, and SAF-T files, authorities are starting to pre-fill draft VAT returns - and expect taxpayers to either confirm the data is correct or provide evidence to dispute the net liability (real-time audits). As a result, managing 100% consistency between your books of account and the authorities' portals is no longer optional, it is the new baseline.                                                                                                                                 

                 

How SAP Enables This - End to End                                                                                                

SAP's approach is to embed compliance directly into business processes, so it runs in the background without disrupting the way teams work. While compliance complexity increases across jurisdictions, no two countries do e-invoicing exactly the same way. SAP solutions abstract this complexity behind the scenes enabling companies to have one process and one solution across countries and business applications (to learn more about SAP Autonomous Suite, check SAP.com). Instead of disrupting existing workflows, it instead leverages digitalization to take automation to new heights. Let’s see how.                                                                                                                       

Regulated customer invoices

When you create a customer invoice in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, contract accounting and invoicing  and SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM) or SAP ERP, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance automatically generates the invoice in the required country-specific format and exchanges it with the relevant government portal. A central cockpit gives you visibility into the latest status and flags errors as they occur. And, if you use any other non-SAP billing systems, you can integrate these customer invoices via API and process in the exact same way of SAP native postings (to learn more, you can watch this replay).

With AI-accelerated error resolution, AI helps you understand errors faster - translating technical error messages into plain language, mapping them to SAP terminology, and suggesting corrective actions. What used to take hours of back-and-forth can now be resolved in seconds. To learn more, you can watch this video.                                                                                                                                                               

Regulated supplier invoices

In countries where regulated distribution networks apply - such as government-managed platforms or 5-corner models - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance downloads supplier invoices centrally and routes them to the right business system automatically. Typically, authorities allow to retrieve invoices only by tax number, irrespective of how many systems you might use for processing. For example, you might have intercompany transactions processed directly in SAP S/4HANA, third-party supplier invoices processed leveraging SAP Business Network and SAP Ariba Invoicing, and travel expenses submitted by employees with Concur Expenses. You can now configure rules based on invoice data, so the right invoice always reaches the right business system. Link to SAP Help documentation.

As authorities step into the flow between suppliers and buyers, you can transform the complexity into an opportunity for greater automation.

  • Straight-through invoice processing. Through native integration with SAP Business Network, SAP Ariba Invoicing, and SAP Invoice Management by OpenText, incoming invoices are used to automatically populate SAP fields for invoice posting. Existing invoice validation workflows - including the 3-way match - continue to run unchanged. Invoices that pass validations are auto posted with no manual intervention, from suppliers to postings in the system of record without interventions. Depending on country-specific requirements, upon completion of invoice processing, authorities are automatically notified of approval, rejection or any other status update, making compliance a native step within your Account Payable operations.
  • Travel expenses without the paperwork. For travel expenses, invoices can be routed to Concur Expenses where they are matched to employees automatically.  Expense items appear pre-populated in the employee's home page - including official invoice IDs like Krajowy System e-Faktur (KSeF) reference number in Poland - eliminating the need to upload receipts and populate expense details manually. From manual scanning of paper receipts to expenses available to be submitted in a claim.

Once invoices are posted, the data feeds into e-reporting and payment processes, including notifying authorities of payment status or embedding official invoice numbers into payment files.

Continuous consistency and submissions

By integrating e‑invoicing into core processes, you can reduce compliance risk and eliminate end‑of‑period crunch. This means moving beyond period-end submissions toward:                                                                                  

  • Real-time consistency checks that compare the business data in your system of record against what tax authorities have on record, resolving discrepancies before they escalate
  • Continuous submissions via electronic documents or reports - aligning authority portals with ERP data throughout the month and giving teams more time to act.                                                                                                                   

The result: audit-ready, any day of the month. Rather than a period-end scramble, compliance activity is spread evenly across the month. When discrepancies appear - whether missing documents or data mismatches - they are flagged, analyzed, and resolved quickly. At the next comparison run, resolved items update automatically. No outstanding differences means nothing to worry about.

To learn more, you can watch this video.                                                                                          

Period-end statutory reporting and tax payments

At period-end, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance keeps your statutory reporting on track: automatically preparing returns in the legally required format, while a central dashboard gives you visibility into deadlines across all entities and countries. Automated deadline tracking and submission reduce the risk of non-compliance and remove manual handoffs.                                                                    

  • Traditional returns prepared from ERP data give tax experts full control: review data, make traceable adjustments fully integrated with accounting records, and drill down from any return line to the underlying business documents. The Manage Tax Items app lets you flexibly slice and dice data, identify documents that need correction, and act on them directly - with audit-proof traceability and no offline documentation required. To learn more, you can watch this video.
  • Draft returns pre-populated by tax authorities can be retrieved directly, reconciled against your ERP data, corrected where needed, and digitally signed - all within the same solution. To learn more, you can watch this video.             

When your reports are ready, a configurable up to two-step approval workflow ensures the right sign offs are in place before submission. For high-volume scenarios, returns can be prepared automatically as deadlines approach and submitted in the background once the review step is complete - keeping your team focused on exceptions, not administration.                    

 

The Bottom Line                                                                                                                           

E-invoicing is not a temporary regulatory hurdle. It is the foundation of a broader shift toward real-time, data-driven tax compliance. Tax authorities are collecting more data, pre-filling tax returns, and closing the gap between what businesses report and what authorities already know.                                                                                                                              

Organizations that treat this as a process transformation, rather than a compliance checkbox, will be better positioned to reduce risk, cut operational effort, and stay ahead of what comes next.

The question is not whether to adapt. It is how fast you can make compliance invisible. More to come on how agentic AI is making Autonomous Compliance a reality - stay tuned.

entry.title: SII Tip for Spain #13: Pro-rata
entry.content:
Welcome to another SII Tip for Spain. The pro-rata (prorrata de IVA) is one of those topics that looks straightforward on paper but hides surprises when SII gets involved. In this post I'll walk you through how pro-rata and SII interact in S/4HANA Public Cloud and Private Cloud, what can go wrong if the configuration is incomplete, and, most importantly, how to fix it.

 

Business Scenario

VAT pro-rata (prorrata del IVA) is defined in LIVA Article 102 and applies to businesses that make deliveries of goods or services, some of them taxable and others VAT-exempt. The typical example is leasing activities: the rental of residential properties is a VAT-exempt transaction with no right to deduct input tax, whereas the rental of commercial premises is a taxable and non-exempt transaction with the right to deduct input tax.

These companies are not allowed to deduct 100% of their input VAT. Instead, they may choose to apply a general or a special pro-rata algorithm. Further details about these algorithms are available in the Tax Agency portal (“Pro rata rule”).

In the simplest case (general), the pro-rata coefficient is the ratio [only turnovers with deductible VAT] / [ total turnover, deductible + not deductible], rounded up to the nearest whole number. This pro-rata percentage is calculated with the information of the year Y1, and will be used as provisional during Y2. At the end of Y2, a recalculation of the definitive pro-rata will take place, and the corresponding adjustments shall be reported in the corresponding VAT return (legal form 303).

For example, a company is dedicated to renting homes and business premises. The operations carried out during year Y1 were the following:

  • Rental of housing: 12,000 euros (input VAT is NOT deductible)
  • Rental of business premises: 32,000 euros (input VAT is deductible)

The “temporary” pro-rata for Y2 will be 32000€/42000€ = 72.72727% => 73%. IMPORTANT: The pro-rata percentage determination has to be carried out outside SAP.

The input VAT supported in year Y2 by expenses used in real estate (both homes and premises) is: Tax base = 6500€, input VAT = 1365€. In this case:

  • Deductible Input VAT = 1365€*73% = 996,45€
  • NON-Deductible Input VAT = 1365€*27% = 368,55€

At the end of Y2, the definitive pro-rata is calculated with the real turnovers of the year. Provided that the definitive pro-rata is 70%, the VAT deductions have to be recalculated. In this case, 1365€*70% = 955,5€. Since there is an excess of deduction of 996,45€-955,5€=40,95€, this this year-end adjustment has to be reported in the legal form 303 in Q4 (but not in SII).

 

Customizing hints in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

In Public Cloud, the pro-rata split is applied automatically when it finds a coefficient entry in the "Define Pro Rata Coefficients" activity (SSCUI 103619) for the tax code. 

In S/4HANA public Cloud Edition, the pro-rata management is described in Pro Rata VAT Processing

1) Define the tax code (FTXP / SSCUI 101016) for country ES:

    • MWVS → VST, 21% (deductible)
    • MWVZ → NVV, 0% placeholder (non-deductible)

2) Define the pro-rata coefficient in SSCUI 103619:

NOTE:  If the tax code is not configured in this table and it defines the NVV Account Key, this value is ignored and the full VAT amount will be posted under the account key VST as  100% deductible (and then CuotaDeducible equals the full CuotaSoportada in SII). When creating the incoming invoice, the system warns at posting time with message RAT007 "Pro rata VAT: Coefficient not defined or out of range (0%-100%)" but does not block the document posting.

3) Assign SII attributes in SSCUI 102558

 

4) Define Final Pro Rata Coefficients for Year-End Adjustment (SSCUI 105523)

 

 

Customizing hints in S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition / ECC

In S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition and in ECC systems, the pro-rata is described in SAP note 801318 - Prorata VAT Solution and in the help portal VAT Pro-Rata Adjustment. The note 801318 is a consulting solution that requires development, so you may need to contact your implementation partner.

As a high level vision of this note:

1) Tax procedure. Copy the standard Spain procedure, add a new condition(step 105, base value routine 907), assign calculation routine 935 to both MWVS (step 120) and MWVZ (step 150).

2) ABAP routines. In a high level view, the deductible/non deductible split is calculated at posting time by the ABAP calculation routine 907 and Routine 935.

3) Condition records  per company code with the annual pro-rata percentage. This is important to calculate the right non-deductible amount.

4) Pro-Rata Validation Entity Values. For the routine 935 to fire, the tax code must be registered in the Pro-Rata Validation Entity values.

NOTE: if the pro-rata realated tax code is not configured properly in this step and/or  the ABAP routines 907 or 935 are incorrectly implemented (e.g., wrong basis value), the deductible and non-deductible rates can fail to add up to a recognized VAT amount by the Tax agency (or be reported as 0%). The Tax Agench SII may issue the error code 1166: "Valor del campo TipoImpositivo no esta incluido en la lista de valores permitidos"  (The value of TipoImpositivo is not a the valid value).

5) Assign SII attributes to the pro-rata tax code (IMG equivalent of SSCUI 102558).

6) Assign the definitive pro-rata at year-end. You can use Transaction Code S_RFIDPTDPR “Definitive Pro-Rata coefficient”  to define the definitive pro-rata percentage and any other periodic (monthly) coefficients if needed:

 

 

 

 

Year-End Adjustments processes

Once the definitive pro-rata is calculated in Q4, it is necessary to adjust the excess/defect of deductions reported based on the “temporary” pro-rata coefficient during the year. These adjustments will be reported in the legal form 303 but not in the SII register books for incoming invoices.

In S/4HANA Public cloud edition,  use the Schedule Tax Jobs Fiori app → Year-End Adjustment of Pro Rata VAT to calculate the adjustment postings.

In S/4PHANA private cloud edition and ECC, you can use transaction S_RFID_PTVPRADPRC00 “Pro-Rata adjustments – Calculation” to calculate the adjustment postings based on the definitive pro-rata

The resulting adjustment postings will be similar to this one:

 

 

SII process

For example an incoming invoice of 1000€ Tax base, 21% VAT, pro-rata coefficient = 50%, is expected to be posted as:

 

Debit

Debit

Debit

Vendor payable

 

1,210 €

Expense

1,000 €

 

Non-deductible VAT (NVV , absorbed in cost)

105 €

 

Input VAT (VST, deductible)

105 €

 

When correctly customized as described, the Financial document for this example should be similar to this one:

The XML reported to SII should be similar to the following:

 

 

Further Reading

In case of additional questions, you can visit the SII help documentation in the following links:

  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in ERP
  • Other SII tips: Spain - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance → Related Links → Community section

Disclaimer

This blog post reflects the author's interpretation of Spanish legal requirements and SAP system behavior as of the publication date. It is intended for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice or consulting guide. For specific guidance, consult your legal or tax advisor and refer to official SAP documentation.

The links to external sources in this blog may be modified at any time externally by the owner without previous notice.

entry.title: Enhanced User List Performance for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Türkiye
entry.content:
Introduction
This blog post introduces a recent enhancement in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Türkiye in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It has been designed to improve the performance and user experience of retrieving registered user list for the  eInvoice and eDelivery/eDespatch processes for customers whose service provider is SOVOS.

Background 
In Türkiye, if your business exchanges electronic invoices and electronic delivery notes, you must maintain an up-to-date list of registered users. This list lets you check whether your trading partner is officially registered with the tax authority as an electronic user. 

Traditionally, if you were using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Türkiye, you relied on two separate applications to retrieve this user list information: 

  • Retrieve Delivery Note Users
  • Retrieve eInvoice Users  

These applications populate the corresponding SSCUI configuration tables, which are consumed by the eInvoice and Delivery Note processes:

  • Default Partner Alias for eInvoicing (ID: 103088) 
  • Define Default Partner Alias for Delivery Notes (103104) 

Limitations of the existing approach: 

  • In the S/4 Public Cloud environment, unlike the Private Cloud, heap memory extension is not possible. 
  • Performance – As the number of registered users in Türkiye continues to grow, the existing solution is not optimized to efficiently process large user lists, which can result in longer execution times and reduced system performance. 

What's New? 
To address the limitations above, SAP has introduced an alternative approach for SOVOS customers to retrieve user lists. User list retrieval is now integrated into the standard Process eDocument Inbound Messages application, the same application that you already use for processing incoming e-documents as part of business operations. 

Key enhancements: 

  • Improved performance — Large user lists are now processed in chunks, enabling efficient retrieval and persistence even for high volumes of registered users. 
  • Unified User Experience – User list retrieval is performed through the same Process eDocument Inbound Messages application used for other inbound e-document processes, providing a consistent and streamlined experience. 
  • Automatic Submission and Persistence – The system automatically requests, receives, processes and persists in the retrieved user list without requiring any additional user action. 
  • Full Traceability – Technical eDocuments are created in the Manage Electronic Documents application, providing complete traceability through detailed application logs and a history tab that displays information such as the request file, file name, and correlation ID. 

Two new message types have been introduced to drive the user list pull through the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application: 

ProcessMessage Type
Delivery NoteTR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL 
eInvoiceTR_INB_USERLIST_PULL 

Prerequisites

  • This enhancement is available only for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
  • As a customer, you must use SOVOS as the service provider for Türkiye Document and Reporting Compliance. 
  • Source type PULL_REQ needs to be activated for the company code in the relevant SSCUI. 
  • SSCUI 105518 Activate Document and Reporting Compliance Features needs to be activated for TR.
  • SSCUI 102637 Activate Additional eDocument Instance Creation needs to be maintained with value CL_EDOC_FACTORY_TR_PULLREQ for PULL_REQ source type.

  • Customers should edit the respective communication arrangement of both eInvoice and eDespatch and deploy the same to get the new API changes. 

New Process to Retrieve the User List 
The user list retrieval process is now fully integrated into the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application. The following steps describe how to execute the process. Subsequent sections explain how to monitor the results using the Manage Electronic Documents, Application Log, and History tab. 

Running the Inbound Message Handler 

  1. Open the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application. 
  2.  Enter the Company Code. 
  3. Enter the Country/Region as Türkiye (TR). 
  4. Select one or both of the following actions: 
    • Get Available Messages – Specify the Received-On date to retrieve new messages from SOVOS. 
    • Process Existing Messages – Specify the Received-On date to process messages that have already been retrieved. 
    You can execute either action independently, run them sequentially (first Get Available Messages, then Process Existing Messages), or execute both actions in a single run. 
  5. Select the appropriate Message Type: 
    • TR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL – Retrieves eDelivery Note users. 
    • TR_INB_USERLIST_PULL – Retrieves eInvoice users. 

  6. Click Execute to start the process. 

The example below illustrates the configuration required to retrieve the eInvoice user list using the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application and the TR_INB_USERLIST_PULL message type. This is provided as an example only. The same configuration steps can be followed to retrieve the eDelivery Note user list by using the TR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL message type. 

Once the process is executed, the system: 

  • Creates technical eDocuments in the Manage Electronic Documents. 
  • Automatically updates the SSCUIs Default Partner Alias for eInvoicing (ID: 103088) and Default Partner Alias for Delivery Notes (103104) with the latest user list data. 
  • Handle auto-submission of technical eDocuments, you do not need to take any further action. 

Scheduling the Job 
To ensure that the user list remains up to date, SAP recommends scheduling the process at a frequency that meets your business requirements. 

Create separate scheduled jobs for: 

  • eInvoice user list retrieval using message type TR_INB_USERLIST_PULL 
  • eDelivery Note user list retrieval using message type TR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL 

The scheduling frequency can be configured according to business needs. 

Monitoring in the eDocument Cockpit 
Once the message handler is executed, the corresponding eDocuments will be visible in the eDocument Cockpit. The two flows can be distinguished by the Type Description: 

ProcessType Description
User List Download Request (eInvoice) Türkiye Incoming Pull Req Inv 
User List Download Request (eDelivery Note) Türkiye Incoming Pull Req Deln 

These are technical eDocuments only. No business documents (such as invoices or delivery notes) are created. The eDocuments serve as tracking records and for audit purposes for the user list retrieval process.

Process Statuses 
The eDocument can have the following key process statuses: 

  • User List Processed — The user list pull and persistence completed successfully. 
  • User List Unprocessed — Something went wrong during the flow. The exact error can be reviewed in the Application Log. 

Application Log 
The Application Log provides a detailed overview of each execution run. For each processed chunk, the log displays the following information: 

  • Total number of users retrieved  
  • The number of users persisted (newly added). 
  • The number of users updated. 
  • Number of users deleted. 

History – Request File 
The history tab of the eDocument provides access to the request file associated with each retrieval run. The request file provides important technical metadata, including: 

  • Correlation ID (only specific to SOVOS) — Enables end-to-end tracing of a specific chunk throughout the processing flow. 
  • File name — Identifies the file received from the SOVOS service. 

Note: The file name is generated and provided by SOVOS. SAP does not control or modify the file name, the system stores the file name exactly as received from the service provider. 

Summary
With this enhancement, the user list retrieval processes in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Türkiye have been integrated into the standard Process eDocument Inbound Messages application.  As a result, customers using SOVOS as their service provider can use the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application as a primary process and keep the existing Retrieve eInvoice Users and Retrieve Delivery Note Users applications as an alternative. 

 Key takeaways: 

  • Use TR_INB_USERLIST_PULL for eInvoice and TR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL for Delivery Note. 
  • SSCUIs Define Default Partner Alias for eInvoicing (ID: 103088) and Define Default Partner Alias for Delivery Notes (103104) are populated automatically when the application is run. 
  • Schedule the report once per process based on your business requirements. 
  • Monitor processing through the technical eDocuments generated in the Manage Electronic Documents, identified by their respective document type descriptions. 
  • Submission and processing are handled automatically by the system, eliminating the need for manual intervention. 

This change provides a more performant, transparent, and unified experience for SOVOS users managing their Türkiye compliance flows.

entry.title: France E-Invoices and E-Reports: The Cloud Edition as Your Approved Platform
entry.content:
SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition is a cloud service running on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). It is a centralized service that allows your business system to exchange electronic documents and submit reports to the tax authorities. Think of it as the following – your business system creates electronic documents and sends them to the cloud edition. Acting as the approved platform (plateforme agréée, PA) for France, the cloud edition then submits the electronic documents and reports to the Public Invoicing Portal (Portail Public de Facturation, PPF), which serves as the intermediary with the tax authorities, and also exchanges electronic documents with your business partners via the Peppol network.

To use the cloud edition, you perform some configuration steps. Let me guide you through it.

Note: Currently, you can only connect with the qualification system of the tax authorities in France. For that, you use the test environment of the cloud edition. This blog post reflects the current state of the solution, in the date of posting. The solution is being continuously updated to reflect the whole legal requirement. Always refer to the SAP Help Portal for the latest information.

 

Step 1: Initial Setup

Before the integration can happen, you need an active subscription for SAP Document and Reporting compliance, cloud edition, in your SAP BTP subaccount. When setting up your subaccount, be aware that due to government legal requirements, the new process for France is available only in the Europe (Rot) - SAP EU Access (cf-eu02) region, where data is processed on SAP-operated infrastructure within Europe.

This should not be confused with the previous France solution that uses only the Peppol Exchange process and is available in all regions.

To be able to create a subaccount in EU02, your global BTP account must be entitled to “EU Access”. If you are entitled to “EU Access”, EU02 is available under the provider “SAP Cloud Infrastructure” when creating a subaccount. If you are not yet entitled to “EU Access”, contact your SAP Account Executive.

When setting up the subaccount, if you are integrating your business system with the cloud edition for the first time, the SAP Help Portal documentation progressively instructs you on the steps that you need to perform.

If you've already integrated your business system with the cloud edition but want to activate the new process for France that is only available in EU02 - which is a different region from what you already have -, you create a new subaccount and go through the integration steps again. In this scenario, specific configuration must be done during the integration steps. I will emphasize this later in this blog post but, for reference, for SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, this involves the RFC connection. For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, this configuration is done when creating the communication system and the communication arrangement.

For more information on the detailed steps, see Initial Setup. 

 

Step 2: Activate Process

For France, you activate the E-Invoices and E-Reports (France) process. The system automatically activates the Peppol Exchange process in the background, you don’t need to activate it separately.

For more information on the detailed steps, see Activating Processes.

 

Step 3: Integrate Your Business System with the Cloud Edition

Depending on your business system, you follow different integration steps. Under the Integration section of the SAP Help Portal, you follow the steps for Integrating All Other Processes and Countries/Regions with <Your Business System>.

For the France-specific scenario, there are a few special details I would like for you to pay attention to, as follows:

  • You need to create two service bindings for the service instance, one of type Other Processes and one of type Peppol Exchange.
  • When creating the service binding of type Peppol Exchange, ALWAYS use OAuth 2.0 as the authentication type.
  • When creating the RFC connection, you use the endpoint drccApiUrl from the service binding of type Other Processes.
  • For SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: If you were already using the cloud edition in another region and are activating France in EU02, you have created a new subaccount. In this case, while going through the integration steps, instead of creating and activating a standard RFC (because it already exists for a different region), you create and activate an RFC connection for France.
  • For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: If you were already using the cloud edition in another region and are activating France in EU02, you have created a new subaccount. In this case, while going through the integration steps, instead of creating a standard communication system and communication arrangement (because they already exist for a different region), you create an additional communication system and communication arrangement for France.
  • The section called Establishing Connection from Cloud Edition to Business System is mandatory for France.

 

Step 4: Enable the Connection Between the Cloud Edition and the Public Invoicing Portal

To be able to exchange your electronic documents, you now need to connect the cloud edition with the Public Invoicing Portal. The French process is based on the 5-corner model, which combines Peppol network and the Public Invoicing Portal. For that reason, you follow steps that are very specific to France, as follows:

  1. You create and register a participant for your company. See Managing Participants for E-Invoices and E-Reports (France). 
    • Currently, you can only connect with the qualification system of the tax authority. This means that you can only use a test SIREN number when registering a participant, not your real company's SIREN number. For more information, see France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.
  2. You create a rule collection to define the default destination to which the cloud edition sends your received electronic documents. See Managing Routing Rules.

 

Additional Information

  • KBA 3724217 - France Mandatory B2B electronic invoicing 2026 - Electronic Document Processing – FAQ
  • Regulatory Change Manager: Mandatory B2B electronic invoicing 2026
  • WorkZone channel: France - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

 

Best regards, 

Aleshane Ghisleri

entry.title: Creating and Activating an RFC Connection for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Prerequisites

  • You have the drccApiUrl endpoint from your service binding. The drccApiUrl is a concatenation of host name and path, in the form https://<host name>/<path>. (See the Getting the URL for a Service Binding section for how to retrieve it.)
  • You have already configured the OAuth 2.0 client in your business system and noted the configuration (profile) name.

Part 1 — Creating the RFC Connection (SM59)

You create a standard or country/region-specific Remote Function Call (RFC) connection.

  1. In your business system, open the RFC Destinations (SM59) transaction.
  2. Choose Create.                                                                                                                                                                                                                  

     

  3. In the Create Destination pop-up, enter a name in the Destination field.
  4. For Connection Type, select G — HTTP Connection to External Server and confirm.

     

  5. On the Technical Settings tab, fill in the fields as follows:

     

  6. Go to the Logon & Security tab.
  7. (Relevant only if you authenticated using OAuth 2.0 and you have SAP S/4HANA 2021 or higher, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.) In the Logon Procedure section, choose OAuth Settings. In the pop-up, for the Profile field, use F4 help to select the configuration name you created when configuring the OAuth 2.0 client.

     

  8. In the Security Options section, set SSL to Active, then choose the PSE according to your authentication method:

    • Client Certificate: choose the SSL Client PSE you created to establish trust between the cloud edition and your business system.

    • OAuth 2.0: choose the SSL Client (Anonymous) PSE.

       

       

  9. Save your entries.
  10. (Optional) To test the connection, choose the Connection Test tab. The expected status depends on your setup:

     

Part 2 — Activating the Standard RFC Connection (GCO_RFC_CONF)

Once the destination exists, you activate it.

  1. In your business system, open the RFC Activation (GCO_RFC_CONF) transaction.
  2. Choose New Entries.                                                                                                                                                                                                              

     

  3. In the RFC Destination field, use the value help to select the destination you created in Part 1.

     

  4.  Confirm the destination and save your entries.

     

  5. Use the Display → Change button to switch to edit mode.
  6. Select the checkbox in the RFC Active column to activate the destination.
  7. Save your entries.

entry.title: SAP Business Network will support regulatory consistent processing of e-Invoices in Spain
entry.content:
Europe’s regulatory landscape is shifting, with countries steadily introducing e-invoicing mandates to tackle the VAT gap. An additional country to add to this digital transformation is Spain. 

The Spanish e-invoicing mandate will be rolled out in different phases, starting 12 months after the publication of the ministerial order, which has been announced for October 2026. At that time, companies with an annual turnover exceeding 8 million euros will have to start issuing and receiving invoices, and notify corresponding statuses.  Companies with an annual turnover of less than 8 million euros get a bit more time and only have to start issuing and receiving invoices 24 months after the final publication of the Ministerial Order, and start exchanging corresponding statuses​ 36 months after that final publication. This phased approach aims to improve fiscal transparency and automation while giving organizations time to align their systems and processes. 

The mandate requires businesses to issue and receive domestic electronic B2B invoices compliant with the European standard EN-16931. To ensure a smooth transition, the regulations allow some flexibility in data formatting — supporting CII, UBL, EDIFACT, and Factura-e — as well as in connectivity, enabling suppliers and buyers to exchange data via private platforms, the official public platform or a hybrid combination. Reporting on payment status and acceptance/rejection must be sent to the public platform within 4 days. 

SAP Business Network will support the outbound flow, i.e. issuing invoices via SAP Business Network, as well as the inbound flow to allow buying customers to receive invoices from both the network and external sources. 

In other words,  suppliers on SAP Business Network can keep sending invoices without major changes,  and keep benefiting from the Network value while being fully compliant with the Spanish mandate. With less manual intervention, suppliers are reducing errors and exceptions and allow for business rules validation and faster processing. This leads to quicker approvals and earlier payments.​ 

To support your readiness, we organize two webcasts in the near future on the Spanish e-invoicing mandate and related SAP Business Network support: 

  • English session: July 8, 2026 at 4:00 PM CEST – Register here 
  • Spanish session: July 8, 2026 at 10:00 AM CEST – Register here 

We are looking forward to welcoming you in these short conferences, which will remain available as recordings after the event.

For additional information on e-invoicing compliance in different countries, please visit our SAP Business Network for Suppliers page.

entry.title: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
France is introducing mandatory electronic invoicing and electronic reporting via an Platform Agreed (plateforme agréée, PA), starting September 2026. This post walks you through how to process electronic invoices using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance as your approved platform (plateforme agréée, PA), covering each step and the corresponding document statuses.

 

Domestic Customer Invoices: Creation

The process begins when you post a customer invoice. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance automatically creates an electronic document under the Domestic Customer Invoice process, with an initial status of eDcoument Created.

At this point, you can preview the XML that will be sent to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition via Display > Display/Preview XML.

From here, you have two options:

  • Display Local XML / Display PDF - the electronic document is sent to the cloud edition, which returns the final XML in local format and PDF visualizations (in both French and English) for your review.   Please note that this integration is asynchronous, so the push integration must be fully configured before use. If any errors occur, they can be reviewed in the Resolve Errors app within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.
  • Submit - either manually or using a background job (EDOC_BACKGROUND).

 

Domestic Customer Invoices: Submission

Upon submission, the electronic document moves through the following statuses:

  1. Sending to Service Requested
  2. Sent to Service
  3. Acknowledged by Service — if the invoice is received successfully in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.  The status updates to Rejected by Service when the cloud edition is unable to accept the electronic document — for example, when resubmitting a file that has already been acknowledged.
  4. Service Error— if any error occurs while processing the invoice - for example if schematron validation fails, PDF creation fails, etc.

If an error occurs, the details will appear in the application log (Goto > Application Log) and, if the issues relate to faulty invoices, you will find schematron validation results in the electronic document history (Goto > History). Look for the file saved with variant ERROR — this file includes all results (including warnings), so focus only on entries classified as fatal. Once you've fixed the root cause, use the Submit button to resubmit.

▎Note: If the document is stuck at Sending to Service Requested, you are most likely missing SAP Notes or configuration settings.

If no errors occurs, the final XML and PDF are generated, pushed back to ERP and stored as attachment in the electronic document history.  At this stage, the electronic document status remains Acknowledged by the Service.

▎Note: These files are pushed into ERP — there is no pull mechanism available, so integration must be fully set up beforehand. If this status transition does not occur, check the Resolve Errors app for issues in the mappings, file creation, or the push to ERP.

 

PPF Registration (Status CDAR_200)

After the invoice in local format is created, a registration status CDAR_200 is sent to the Public Invoicing Portal (Portail Public de Facturation, PPF). This registration request is also returned to ERP and stored in the electronic document history.

You can track the PPF status in the PPF Status and PPF Status Description fields in the EDOC_COCKPIT or the Manage Electronic Documents app. After the CDAR_200 registration is created and transmitted, the PPF Status Description updates to PPF Request Generated and, subsequently, to Registered with PPF.

PPF then sends an acknowledgement that is processed through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition and integrated back to ERP, updating the field to PPF Acknowledgement Received. Upon processing of PPF acknowledgement, the electronic document status is updated to Accepted by the Service.

▎Important: If the PPF acknowledgement is not received (i.e., the PPF Status Description does not change to PPF Acknowledgement Received), neither Peppol transmission to the recipient nor Flow 1 to PPF are initiated.

 

Domestic Customer Invoices: Exchange

Invoice sent to recipient via Peppol Network

After acknowledgement of 200 registration, the document is sent to recipient via Peppol Network. The exchange status is tracked through the electronic document status:

Status

Code

Rejected by Receiver Platform

CDAR_213

Received by Receiver Platform

CDAR_202

Received by Customer    

CDAR_203

Acknowledged by Customer

CDAR_204

▎Note:  Please refer to Domestic Supplier Invoices: Receipt to understand when these CDAR statuses are sent for buyers using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

▎Note: If the recipient PA does not receive (acknowledge) the invoice, the status remains Accepted by Service, even if the document has already been transmitted via Peppol Network. To check whether transmission via Peppol Network was initiated, use the app Monitor Documents app (Peppol monitor) in the cloud edition. If any failure occurred, the document is in status Sending failed. Any issues with pushing recipient acknowledgements can also be investigated in the Monitor Documents app.

At this stage, the invoice can be processed from a business perspective.

Depending on buyer responses, the following optional and required statuses may follow:

Status

Code

Required?

Accepted by Customer          

CDAR_205

Optional

Partially Accepted by Customer

CDAR_206

Optional

Disputed by Customer          

CDAR_207

Optional

Suspended by Customer         

CDAR_208

Optional

Completed

CDAR_209

Optional (buyer can mark documents as complete that have been suspended)

Rejected by Customer          

CDAR_210

Mandatory

Payment Sent by Customer      

CDAR_211

Optional

 

Invoice tax data sent to PPF (Flow 1)

After acknowledgement, PPF has up to 1 hour to reject the document. If no rejection is received within that window, the document is deemed accepted. At the earliest point between acceptance, rejection, or the 1-hour timeout, the electronic document is updated and transmission of Flow 1 to PPF is initiated.

The Flow 1 sends tax data to PPF and the exchange status is tracked in the PPF Status and PPF Status Description fields:

  • Flow 1 Data Submitted
  • Flow 1 Submission Failed
  • Flow 1 Data Accepted
  • Flow 1 Data Rejected

▎Note: If Flow 1 fails, reprocessing is not currently supported. Further options are being evaluated.

 

Domestic Supplier Invoices: Receipt

Supplier invoices are received in the SAP business system via Peppol Network through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, which acts as the approved platform.

Before you begin:

  • Routing rules are mandatory, even if you use only one business system. See the SAP Help documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/managing-routing-rules?locale=en-US) for setup guidance.
  • If errors occur during receipt:
    • Check the app Resolve Errors in the cloud edition. Here you can find invoices that could not be pushed to the business system.
    • If no errors seen in Resolve Errors app, use the Monitor Documents app (Peppol Monitor) in the cloud edition to view detailed logs and reprocess failed documents.

When an electronic invoice is received, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition first validates the document. If validation fails, one of the following CDAR statuses is returned to the buyer depending on which check failed:

  • Routing error (CDAR_221) – This is triggered when the address of invoice recipient is not registered with SAP as PA (Endpoint ID doesn't match PartyLegalEntity ID - SIREN).
  • Inadmissible (CDAR_501) – This is sent if XSD Schema validation fails (syntax error).
  • Rejected by Receiver Platform (CDAR_213) - This is sent if schematron validation fails (semantic error).

▎Note:  Even if validations fail, the electronic invoice will appear in the Monitor Documents app (Peppol Monitor) in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition with a status of Pushed. Monitoring of rejection statuses is a planned enhancement, timeline is currently under assessment. Please refer to Work Zone for the latest updates.

If all validations pass, the invoice is accepted and CDAR_202 (Received by Receiver Platform) is sent to the buyer.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition then converts the supplier invoice and generates the PDF visualization, in both French and English. Once successful, the invoice is pushed to the business system and CDAR_203 (Received by Customer) is sent to the buyer.

Any errors related to routing rules or the push to the business system can be investigated and resolved in the Resolve Errors app in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

▎Note: In the Monitor Documents app (Peppol Monitor), these invoices appear as Document Pushed in green. This status indicates the document has been pushed for processing after CDAR_203 — it does not confirm that the invoice has been successfully pushed to the business system.

The invoice is created asynchronously in the target business system — SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Business Network, or SAP Ariba Invoicing.

For SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the invoice is created as an electronic document under the Domestic Supplier Invoices process, and CDAR_204 (Acknowledged by Customer) is sent to the buyer. In the electronic document history, you can find the original invoice sent by the supplier, the invoice converted to UBL format and PDF visualizations (French and English). 

If the acknowledgement is not processed automatically, users can trigger it manually via EDOC_COCKPIT (or the Manage Electronic Documents app).

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance also offers integration with SAP Invoice Management by OpenText, and supports extensibility for automating invoice posting with other solutions.

For more details on processing supplier invoices, see these SAP Community blog posts:

  • I received an electronic supplier invoice, what now? 
  • Configuring Reason Codes and Action Codes for France B2B Supplier Invoices.

 

Payment Received for Domestic Customer Invoices

For invoices related to services where VAT is due based on payment (as opposed to invoice date), sellers must report a Payment Received status (CDAR_212).

When a customer invoice is cleared, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance automatically creates an electronic document under the Domestic Customer Payment process. A few things to note:

  • If a single payment clears multiple invoices, one electronic document is created per cleared invoice.
  • For partial payments, each instalment creates a separate electronic document, but the customer invoice status is not updated until the invoice is fully paid.

The processing flow mirrors the customer invoice process with the electronic documents being updated with following statuses:

  1. Sending to Service Requested
  2. Sent to Service
  3. Acknowledged by Service — if the invoice is received successfully. The status updates to Rejected by Service when SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition is unable to accept the electronic document — for example, when resubmitting a file that has already been acknowledged.
  4. Error at Service — if any error occurs while processing the electronic document for creation of CDAR_212.

Once the cloud edition acknowledges the electronic documents, the final XML format (CDAR_212) is generated and pushed back to ERP. At this point, CDAR_212 is sent to both the seller and PPF.

The PPF status is tracked through dedicated fields, transitioning from F_WAIT_ACK (Wait for Ack. from PPF) to F_ACK_REC (Received Ack. from PPF) and finally F_SENT (Sent to PPF), which indicates the CDAR_212 has been accepted by PPF.

The electronic document status is updated as follow:

  • Once PPF acknowledges CDAR_212, the electronic document status is updated to Accepted by Service.
  • When the seller accepts the status update, the status changes to Accepted by Customer.
  • Once both seller and PPF have accepted CDAR_212, the electronic document status is further updated to Payment Received Status Sent (final status).
  • If PPF accepts CDAR_212 before the seller, the PPF acceptance is recorded in the dedicated fields and held until the seller’s acceptance is received. Once the seller sends their acceptance, the electronic document status is updated to Payment Received Status Sent (final status).

If the payment clears the total outstanding balance of the customer invoice (i.e., it’s not a partial payment), the transition to Payment Received Status Sent  also updates the status of original electronic invoice to Payment received. 

If a failure occurs, it will be either a rejection or a technical error from PPF or the Receiver Platform (Buyer’s PA):

  • Technical error — the electronic document status is set to Error at Service. Use the Retry button in the eDocument Cockpit to reprocess.
  • Rejected by PPF — the electronic document status is set to Rejected by Service and the PPF status description is Rejected by PPF. The document must be resubmitted.
  •  Rejected by Receiver Platform (Buyer’s PA) — the electronic document status is set to Rejected by Customer. The document must be resubmitted.

 

  Summary: CDAR Statuses

Sent by seller

Status

Code

Recipient

Registered

CDAR_200

PPF only

Payment Received

CDAR_212

PPF and Buyer

Completed

CDAR_209

Buyer only

 

Sent by buyer

Status

Code

Recipient

Accepted by Customer          

CDAR_205

Seller only

Partially Accepted by Customer

CDAR_206

Seller only

Disputed by Customer          

CDAR_207

Seller only

Suspended by Customer         

CDAR_208

Seller only

Rejected by Customer          

CDAR_210

PPF and Seller

Payment Sent by Customer      

CDAR_211

Seller only

▎ Note: If an invoice is Rejected by Receiving Platform (CDAR_213), this must also be reported to both PPF and the Seller.

 

To see the end-to-end flow in action - system demo showcasing the various steps and electronic document statuses - please watch the replay of the webinar held on July 6, 2026: https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/5398959/572E6C7DE0C5253DDCF8463CCC7350CB.

entry.title: SAP Ariba Invoicing: France e-invoicing mandate from September 2026
entry.content:
Integration of French invoices via SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (PA)

SAP Ariba Invoicing, in conjunction with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, offers an integrated and automated solution for SAP customers to comply with the French e-invoicing mandate, which will be rolled out in September 2026. Both the incoming invoices and the mandatory lifecycle statuses (REFUSÉE B2B) are supported:

The joint solution integrates invoices issued by French suppliers from their selected Plateforme Agréée (PA) and receives them into SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, which acts as the technology for SAP as a certified provider ( link ). The solution supports buyer-mandated lifecycle statuses, such as Refused, and sends these status updates back to the supplier's PA and the Public Platform (PPF).

Integration of French invoices via a third-party PA

Customers that do not use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance as their buyer PA can still comply with the French e-invoicing mandate by using the standard SAP Ariba Invoicing APIs. The receipt of invoices and the mandatory lifecycle statuses (B2B refusé) are supported by the solution.

This alternative option requires the following integration steps:

  1. Integration from the buyer PA: E-invoices received from the buyer's PA in any of the three formats compliant with the European Norm (UBL, CII, or Factur-X) must be converted or mapped to the SAP Ariba Invoicing invoice format by using the Supplier Invoices with Structured Data API.

  2. The rejection (refusal) status of rejected (refused) invoices in SAP Ariba Invoicing must be channelled back to the buyer’s PA. This can be done through integration by leveraging the Invoice Status Changes API. This service triggers calls to an external API each time there is an invoice status or payment status change from SAP Ariba Invoicing. In the context of the French e-invoicing mandate, only invoices moving to the Rejected status need to be channelled back to the buyer PA along with their rejection reason.

For more details on activating and using these SAP Ariba Invoicing APIs, please refer to the SAP Ariba Invoicing documentation for your deployment type:

  • Customer-managed (4N6): API Guide or
  • SAP-managed / Next-Gen SAP Ariba Invoicing (7YH): API Guide

entry.title: SAP 会計ソリューションを学ぶならまずはこちら！公開コンテンツまとめ
entry.content:
はじめに

「SAP会計には色々なソリューションがあるって聞くけど、結局どこから学べばよいのだろう？」
会計・財務領域に関わる方であれば、一度はそう感じたことがあるかもしれません。
SAPでは、財務会計、管理会計、連結会計、財務資金管理など、幅広い会計ソリューションを提供しています。
一方で、領域が広いからこそ、全体像をつかむのは簡単ではありません。

そこで本記事では、SAPの会計ソリューションをこれから学びたい方に向けて、公開動画コンテンツをテーマ別に整理しました。
各動画は15分程度で要点を押さえられるため、まずは気になるテーマから気軽にご覧いただけます。

また、2026年のSapphireで発表されたAutonomous EnterpriseやAIエージェント関連のコンテンツなど、今後順次追加していく予定です。

ぜひブックマークいただき、SAP 会計ソリューションの学習や情報収集にご活用ください。

 

SAP会計ソリューション早わかり（プレイリスト） 

SAPでは、財務会計、財務資金管理、管理会計、連結会計、コンプライアンス対応など、企業の経理/財務業務を幅広く支援しています。
また、それぞれのソリューションは独立して存在するものではなく、統合されたデータ基盤の上で連携しながら、経営情報の可視化や業務効率化、経営管理の高度化を実現します。 

本記事では、各業務領域におけるSAPソリューションの役割や、どのような課題を解決できるのかを、動画コンテンツとともにご紹介します。
ご自身の業務や関心のあるテーマにあわせてコンテンツを深掘りしていただければ幸いです。

本コンテンツで学べること

  • SAP 会計ソリューション全体像
  • 各ソリューションの役割と位置づけ
  • 自社課題とソリューションの関連性
  • 個別テーマ動画を見る前に押さえておきたいポイント

こんな方におすすめ

  • SAPの会計領域を初めて学ぶ方
  • 会計・財務領域全体を俯瞰したい方
  • SAP導入を検討している企業の業務部門・IT部門の方

 

アジェンダ (2026.07 現在)　※クリックすると各セクションにジャンプします。

  1. 債権・債務管理
  2. 総勘定元帳・決算
  3. 固定資産・リース管理
  4. 財務資金管理
  5. 管理会計
  6. 連結会計
  7. グループ経営管理
  8. デジタルインボイス・法定申告対応

 

1. 債権・債務管理

債権・債務管理は、企業活動における請求・支払・回収業務を支える重要な領域です。
多くの企業では、販売システム・購買システム・会計システムが分断されており、二重入力やデータ不整合、属人化した業務が課題となっています。

SAPでは、販売・購買から会計までを統合的に管理することで、業務標準化と効率化を実現します。

 

この動画で学べること

  •  債権・債務管理における主な業務課題とその対処法
  •  販売・購買・会計を統合管理する考え方
  •  請求・支払・入金消込業務の標準化・効率化のアプローチ
  •  AIや自動化を活用した経理業務高度化のポイント

こんな方におすすめ

  • 債権・債務管理業務の効率化や標準化に関心がある方
  • 販売・購買・会計をつなげた業務プロセスを理解したい方
  • 入金消込や支払処理の自動化を検討している方
  • 経理業務におけるAI活用に関心がある方

▶ 動画リンク：SAP 会計ソリューション 債権債務管理概要
 ※ 画像をクリックすると、該当動画ページに移動します。

 

2. 総勘定元帳・決算

決算業務は、企業の財務情報を取りまとめ、経営判断や外部報告につながる重要な業務です。
しかし実際には、業務システムと会計システムの不整合、Excelを活用した手作業、属人化した業務プロセスなどにより、多くの企業が決算早期化やガバナンス強化に課題を抱えています。

SAPでは、業務と会計をリアルタイムに連携するとともに、決算タスクの標準化や進捗の可視化、自動化を実現することで、決算業務の効率化とガバナンス強化を支援します。

この動画で学べること

  • 総勘定元帳・決算業務における主な業務課題とその対処法
  • 業務データと会計データをリアルタイムに連携する考え方
  • 決算業務の標準化・自動化による効率化のアプローチ
  • 決算タスクの可視化やガバナンス強化のポイント

こんな方におすすめ

  • 月次・年次決算の効率化や早期化に関心がある方
  • Excelや手作業に依存した決算業務を見直したい方
  • 決算業務の属人化解消や標準化を進めたい方
  • 決算業務の進捗管理やガバナンスを強化したい方

▶ 動画リンク：SAP 会計ソリューション 単体決算・決算管理概要
 ※ 画像をクリックすると、該当動画ページに移動します。

 

3. 固定資産・リース管理

固定資産管理やリース管理では、固定資産管理システムと会計システムの分断による二重入力やデータ照合作業、リース会計基準への対応、設備投資管理の高度化など、多くの企業が課題を抱えています。

SAPでは、固定資産管理と会計を統合した仕組みを提供することで、業務領域間の整合性を確保しながら、設備投資管理やリース管理を含めた幅広い業務を一元的に管理することができます。

この動画で学べること

  • 固定資産管理やリース管理における主な業務課題とその対処法
  • 固定資産管理と一般会計を統合管理する考え方
  • 設備投資管理やリース管理を一元化するアプローチ
  • AIや最新技術を活用した固定資産管理業務の効率化のポイント

こんな方におすすめ

  • 固定資産管理業務の効率化に関心がある方
  • 固定資産管理と一般会計のシステム間連携を見直したい方
  • リース会計のシステム対応を検討されている方
  • AIを活用した経理業務・固定資産管理業務の効率化に関心がある方

▶ 動画リンク：SAP 会計ソリューション 固定資産/リース管理概要
 ※ 画像をクリックすると、該当動画ページに移動します。

さらに詳しく学びたい方へ
より詳しく学びたい方は、リース会計基準への対応や契約管理の考え方を解説したセミナー動画もぜひご覧ください。
▶ リース管理セミナー動画：【セミナー録画】SAPリース会計ソリューションセミナー第1回概要編
 ※ 画像をクリックすると、該当動画ページに移動します。

 

4. 財務資金管理

企業を取り巻く環境が複雑化する中、財務部門には単なる資金繰り管理だけでなく、グループ全体の資金効率向上やリスク管理、ガバナンス強化が求められています。一方で、多くの企業では「海外子会社の資金状況が見えない」「資金運用が手作業中心」「グローバルで資金を有効活用できていない」といった課題を抱えています。

SAPでは、Cash Management、Treasury and Risk Management、Multi-Bank Connectivity、Advanced Payment Management、Tauliaなどのソリューションを組み合わせることで、財務資金管理業務全体の高度化を支援します。

この動画で学べること

  • 財務資金管理における主な業務課題とその対処法
  • グループ全体の資金状況を可視化する考え方
  • 資金繰り、銀行接続、支払管理、財務リスク管理等の高度化アプローチ
  • 運転資本管理やFintechソリューション活用のポイント

こんな方におすすめ

  • グループ会社を含めた資金状況をタイムリーに把握したい方
  • 資金管理や支払業務の効率化・標準化に関心がある方
  • 為替・金利などの財務リスク管理を強化したい方
  • 手元資金の有効活用や運転資本改善に取り組みたい方

▶ 動画リンク：SAP 会計ソリューション 財務資金管理概要
 ※ 画像をクリックすると、該当動画ページに移動します。

さらに詳しく学びたい方へ
より詳しく学びたい方には、銀行管理、資金繰り管理、財務リスク管理、ペイメントファクトリーなどのテーマを個別に解説した詳細編動画になります。
各テーマの業務課題やSAPによる解決アプローチを、より深く理解いただけます。

▶ 財務資金管理 詳細編動画：SAP会計ソリューション 財務資金管理概要 デジタルトレジャリー実現編
 ※ 画像をクリックすると、該当動画ページに移動します。



また、実際にSAP Treasuryソリューションを活用して財務業務の高度化に取り組まれたお客様事例も公開しています。
具体的な活用イメージや導入効果を知りたい方は、ぜひあわせてご覧ください。

▶ お客様事例動画：日立製作所 グローバル財務資金リスク管理 変革事例
 ※ 画像をクリックすると、該当動画ページに移動します。

 

5. 管理会計

管理会計の目的は、部門別・製品別・顧客別などのさまざまな切り口で収益性を把握し、経営判断につなげることです。
しかし実際には、原価情報や収益情報がリアルタイムに把握できず、差異分析や改善アクションが後手に回るケースも少なくありません。

SAPでは、業務データと会計データを統合管理することで、部門別費用や製造原価、製品別・顧客別収益性をリアルタイムに可視化し、迅速な経営判断を支援します。

この動画で学べること

  • 管理会計における主な業務課題とその対処法
  • 部門別・製品別・顧客別に収益性を把握する考え方
  • 原価管理や予算実績管理を高度化するアプローチ
  • データに基づく経営判断を支える仕組み

こんな方におすすめ

  • 原価管理や収益性分析の高度化に関心がある方
  • 予算実績管理や差異分析をタイムリーに行いたい方
  • 製品別・顧客別の採算性を可視化したい方
  • データに基づく経営判断や意思決定を強化したい方

▶ 動画リンク：SAP 会計ソリューション 管理会計概要 管理会計業務の高度化を実現するSAPソリューション 全体概要編
 ※ 画像をクリックすると、該当動画ページに移動します。

 

6. 連結会計

企業のグローバル化やM&Aの進展により、連結決算業務の重要性はますます高まっています。
一方で、グループ会社ごとにシステムやデータ粒度が異なることで、データ収集やグループ内取引の照合に多くの時間を要し、グループ全体の経営状況をタイムリーに把握できない企業も少なくありません。

SAPでは、単体会計と連結会計を統合的に管理することで、データ収集の効率化や連結決算の早期化に加え、制度連結・管理連結の両面からグループ経営の高度化を支援します。

この動画で学べること

  • 連結会計における主な業務課題とその対処法
  • SAP Group Reportingによる連結決算の全体像
  • グループ会社からのデータ収集を効率化する考え方
  • 内部取引照合や連結処理を標準化・効率化するアプローチ
  • 制度連結・管理連結の高度化に向けたポイント

こんな方におすすめ

  • 連結決算業務の効率化や早期化に関心がある方
  • グループ会社からのデータ収集負荷を軽減したい方
  • 内部取引照合や連結処理の標準化・効率化を進めたい方
  • 制度連結だけでなく管理連結の高度化を検討している方
  • 連結数値の根拠確認や要因分析を効率化したい方

▶ 動画リンク：SAP 会計ソリューション 連結決算概要 連結決算早期化・グループ企業価値向上の実現 全体概要編

 

7. グループ経営管理

グローバル化やM&Aの進展に伴い、多くの企業では複数のERPや会計システムが混在し、グループ全体の経営状況をタイムリーに把握することが難しくなっています。また、各社ごとに異なるKPIや業務プロセス、システムを利用していることで、グループ横断での経営管理や業務改善が進めづらいという課題もあります。

SAPでは、Central Financeをはじめとするソリューションを活用し、既存システムを活かしながら会計データを集約するとともに、グループ共通の経営基盤を構築することで、経営管理の高度化を支援します。

この動画で学べること

  • グループ経営管理における主な業務課題とその対処法
  • 複数ERP・会計システムのデータを統合する考え方
  • グループ横断で経営状況を可視化するアプローチ
  • グループ共通の経営管理基盤を構築するポイント

こんな方におすすめ

  • グループ全体の経営状況をタイムリーに把握したい方
  • 複数システムに分散した会計データの統合を検討している方
  • グループ共通のKPIや業務プロセスの標準化に関心がある方
  • 既存システムを活かしながら経営管理基盤を整備したい方

▶ 動画リンク：SAP 会計ソリューション グループ経営管理 グループ・グローバル経営を実現するデータドリブン経営基盤 全体概要編

 

8. デジタルインボイス・法定申告対応（DRC）

近年、各国で電子インボイスや税務デジタル化への対応が進んでいます。日本においてもインボイス制度や電子帳簿保存法への対応が求められており、制度変更への継続的な対応が重要になっています。

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance（DRC）は、こうした法制度対応を支援するソリューションです。

この動画で学べること

  • デジタルインボイス・法定申告対応における主な業務課題とその対処法
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance（DRC）の全体像
  • 電子インボイスや法定申告対応を効率化するアプローチ
  • 各国の法制度変更に継続的に対応するためのポイント

こんな方におすすめ

  • デジタルインボイス対応を進めたい方
  • 法定申告業務の効率化や標準化に関心がある方
  • グローバルで税務・法制度対応を管理したい方
  • 制度変更への継続的な対応負荷を軽減したい方
  • 税務・法制度へのコンプライアンス対応を向上したい方

まとめ

SAPの会計領域は、債権・債務管理、総勘定元帳・決算、固定資産・リース管理、財務資金管理、管理会計、連結会計、グループ経営管理、デジタルインボイス・法定申告対応（DRC）など、企業の経理・財務業務の幅広い領域をカバーしています。
また、それぞれのソリューションは独立して存在するものではなく、統合されたデータ基盤の上で連携しながら、業務効率化やガバナンス強化、さらには経営判断の高度化を支えています。

最後に、本記事がSAP 会計ソリューションを学ぶための入口として、皆様の情報収集や業務改革のヒントになれば幸いです。

ご質問やご要望がございましたら、ぜひコメント欄にてお知らせください。
